title: Classic Game Postmortem: DOOM
creator: Tom Hall and John Romero
description: Two original id Software designers reconstruct DOOM's development, showing early material and recounting the decisions that shaped the 1993 game.
useful: Useful when studying how a small team, technical constraints, and changing scope affected a landmark project.
audience: Developers and historians interested in how influential games emerge from small teams and changing plans.
learning: Trace DOOM from early plans to the released game
See how tools and technology affected level design
Recognize the effect of team structure and scope changes
